{"problem_standardized_math":"Assume that $B$ is positive definite. In the double-dogleg method, a path consisting of three line segments is constructed from the origin to the full step, determined by the four points\n\n- the origin,\n- the unconstrained Cauchy step $p^c = -\\dfrac{g^Tg}{g^TBg}\\,g$,\n- a fraction of the full step $\\bar{\\gamma}p^B = -\\bar{\\gamma}B^{-1}g$, where $\\bar{\\gamma} \\in (\\gamma,1]$ and $\\gamma$ is defined in the previous question,\n- the full step $p^B = -B^{-1}g$.\n\nShow that $\\|p\\|$ increases monotonically along this path."}
